name: Valibot
what_it_does: Modular, tree-shakeable TypeScript schema validation library using pipe() composition and v() schema functions, designed for minimal bundle footprint.
use_cases: Validate data in bundle-size-sensitive environments (edge functions, browsers) where Zod's monolithic size is prohibitive
Compose validation pipelines with pipe() to apply sequential transformations and checks on a single value type
Use v.parse() and v.safeParse() with the same discriminated-union result pattern familiar from Zod for structured error handling
Infer TypeScript types from Valibot schemas with v.InferOutput<typeof schema> to maintain type-runtime parity
Incrementally adopt modular validation by importing only the specific validators needed (string, email, minLength) rather than the full library
not_for: Teams standardized on Zod who have no bundle-size constraints — the API differences add friction without clear benefit
Projects requiring a large ecosystem of third-party integrations — Zod has significantly more adapter libraries and framework integrations
Production use where the pre-1.0 versioning indicates API stability is not yet guaranteed
best_when: Building for edge runtimes, browser bundles, or any context where per-function tree-shaking of validation code meaningfully reduces payload size.
avoid_when: Ecosystem compatibility matters more than bundle size — Zod's integrations with tRPC, React Hook Form, and other libraries are much broader.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: ValiError includes a structured issues array with path and message similar to ZodError — machine-readable, though the API evolves frequently in pre-1.0 releases.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Pure parsing function with no side effects — deterministic output for any given input.
known_agent_gotchas: Valibot is pre-1.0 and has breaking API changes between minor versions — agents depending on it should pin exact versions
pipe() wraps a base schema with actions; applying an action to the wrong base type (e.g., minLength on a number) fails silently in some versions
v.parse() throws a ValiError — agents must use v.safeParse() for non-throwing validation and check the .success discriminant
Tree-shaking benefits require a bundler (Vite, esbuild, Webpack) — agents running in Node.js without bundling import the same code as Zod
Ecosystem adapter support (tRPC, React Hook Form, Drizzle) is still growing — agents scaffolding integrations may find adapters missing or unofficial

security_score_components.security_notes: Pure validation library with no network access or external dependencies beyond optional integrations — minimal attack surface.
